beats.text: Two wired channels side by side: twisted-pair on the left, coaxial on the right.
Twisted-pair's bandwidth label reads one hundred kilohertz. A crosstalk warning icon pulses between its wires — interference leaking between pairs.
Coaxial's bandwidth label reads one hundred megahertz. The crosstalk icon disappears — the shielded core blocks it completely.
But zoom out on the coaxial cable. Repeater boxes appear every fixed interval along the line — extra hardware just to keep the signal alive.
A scale appears: bandwidth on one side, complexity on the other. Higher bandwidth tips the scale — but complexity rises with it. That's the trade-off.
